BRIDGE (Track A -> Track B): to the best of our knowledge the peer-reviewed literature contains no established physical mechanism for the unresolved residue; existing exotic-propulsion theory (warp/wormhole/metamaterial; AAWSAP advanced-propulsion grey-literature) is speculative and outside peer review. A documented absence, not a conjecture; it closes Section 1 and opens Track B
Taking Knuth's stated Tic Tac drop energy 4.3e11 J at face value, it equals ~100 tons of TNT (computed 102.8)
If the reported peak speed (~46,000 mph) is taken at face value, Loeb-Kirkpatrick eq (2) requires an optical fireball of order ~5.3 TW (A=10 m^2, sea-level density) — a conspicuous signature
At the reported peak speed the Mach number is ~60, so known physics requires a strong shock, sonic boom, and thermal signature

Because LK fireball luminosity scales as distance^5 and inferred speed as distance, a 3x range error swings the inferred signature by 3^5 = 243x — modest range error collapses or inflates the apparent anomaly
Public kinematic data do not establish non-human/exotic propulsion for any UAP

Discriminating signature: claimed >>Mach-1 / high-g flight sustained WITHOUT the LK-required signatures (fireball, ionization/RF, sonic boom, thermal) is the falsifiable discriminator; current evidence (GoFast parallax, AARO sensor-artifact findings, range sensitivity A04) favors velocity overestimation; only signature-absence under independently-verified range would point beyond known physics
